Code Reference¶

Here you will find reference to the specific functions used in the RAFIKI-CGM pacakge.

pipeline.py¶

pipeline.run_sz(config)¶

Generates SZ data products

Parameters:

config (yaml) – input yaml file

pipeline.run_xray(config)¶

Generates X-ray data products

Parameters:

config (yaml) – input yaml file

catalog.py¶

catalog.select_by_ranges(config)¶

Loads the parameters for generating a mock galaxy sample catalog, used only if selection.method is set to range

Parameters:

config (yaml) – input yaml files

Returns:

index_sample-array of galaxy indices in the RAFIKI-CGM catalog file that will be used for our mock galaxy sample

Return type:

np.ndarray

catalog.select_by_matching(config)¶

Load the galaxy sample catalog from the observations, used only if selection.method is set to matching

Parameters:

config (yaml) – input yaml file

Returns:

Indices for galaxy sample selected to match the observed distribution

Return type:

np.ndarray

catalog.load_catalog(config)¶

Loads the catalog of mock galaxy properties for comparison

Parameters:

config (yaml) – input yaml files

Returns:

stell-Array of galaxy stellar masses (solar units)

Returns:

halo-Array of galaxy halo masses (solar units)

Returns:

rad-Array of galaxy virial radii (kpc)

Returns:

age-Array of galaxy ages (Gyr)

Returns:

sfr-Array of galaxy star formation rates (solar units/yr)

Returns:

ssfr-Array of galaxy specific star formation rates (Gyr^-1)

Returns:

frb_locs-Array of galaxy locations in tSZ FRB map (pixel units)

Returns:

central-Boolean flag if galaxies are central in their halo

Return type:

np.ndarray

sz.py¶

sz.determining_frb_size(box_size, z, comov, angular_res)¶

Tells you how many pixels you want in your image for a given angular resolution

Parameters:

  • box_size (float) – Size of the box in units of Mpccm/h

  • z (float) – redshift of your snapshot

  • comov (float) – Comoving distance in kpc. Suggested to use Ned Wright’s cosmology calculator to determine

  • angular_res (float) – The angular size of each pixel you want in your frb in units of arcseconds

Returns:

How many pixels you want to make your frb to achieve the desired angular resolution

Return type:

int

sz.cut_stamps(config, index_sample)¶

Cut out stamps of Compton-y map around every galaxy in the sample. Return nested array of the stamps

Parameters:

  • config (yaml) – input yaml file

  • index_sample (np.ndarray) – array of galaxy indices in the RAFIKI-CGM catalog file that will be used for our mock galaxy sample

Returns:

stamps-nested array of 2D stamps centered around each galaxy of interest

Return type:

np.ndarray

sz.cropping_sz_x(sz_dat, xs, ys, zs, sample, width, frb)¶

Generates a nested 2D array of SZ data in the regions around our sampled galaxies using a data box projected in the x-direction

Parameters:

  • sz_dat (list[float]) – 2D array of projected Compton-y map

  • xs (list[float]) – x-coordinates of locations of galaxies in your box, adjusted to match the pixel frb

  • ys (list[float]) – y-coordinates of locations of galaxies in your box, adjusted to match the pixel frb

  • zs (list[float]) – z-coordinates of locations of galaxies in your box, adjuste to match the pixel frb

  • sample (list[int]) – list of index locations of the galaxies we want to sample (output of sorting)

  • width (int) – the pixel size we want to crop around the galaxies

  • frb (int) – pixel size of the SZ data used

Returns:

lists-Nested array of SZ data for the regions around our sample gaalxies

Return type:

list[float]

sz.cropping_sz_y(sz_dat, xs, ys, zs, sample, width, frb)¶

Generates a nested 2D array of SZ data in the regions around our sampled galaxies using a data box projected in the y-direction

Parameters:

  • sz_dat (list[float]) – 2D array of projected Compton-y map

  • xs (list[float]) – x-coordinates of locations of galaxies in your box, adjusted to match the pixel frb

  • ys (list[float]) – y-coordinates of locations of galaxies in your box, adjusted to match the pixel frb

  • zs (list[float]) – z-coordinates of locations of galaxies in your box, adjuste to match the pixel frb

  • sample (list[int]) – list of index locations of the galaxies we want to sample (output of sorting)

  • width (int) – the pixel size we want to crop around the galaxies

  • frb (int) – pixel size of the SZ data used

Returns:

lists-Nested array of SZ data for the regions around our sample gaalxies

Return type:

list[float]

sz.cropping_sz_z(sz_dat, xs, ys, zs, sample, width, frb)¶

Generates a nested 2D array of SZ data in the regions around our sampled galaxies using a data box projected in the z-direction

Parameters:

  • sz_dat (list[float]) – 2D array of projected Compton-y map

  • xs (list[float]) – x-coordinates of locations of galaxies in your box, adjusted to match the pixel frb

  • ys (list[float]) – y-coordinates of locations of galaxies in your box, adjusted to match the pixel frb

  • zs (list[float]) – z-coordinates of locations of galaxies in your box, adjuste to match the pixel frb

  • sample (list[int]) – list of index locations of the galaxies we want to sample (output of sorting)

  • width (int) – the pixel size we want to crop around the galaxies

  • frb (int) – pixel size of the SZ data used

Returns:

lists-Nested array of SZ data for the regions around our sample gaalxies

Return type:

list[float]

sz.azimuthalAverage(image, center=None)¶

Calculates the azimuthally averaged radial profile. Taken from with some alterations https://github.com/mkolopanis/python/blob/master/radialProfile.py

Parameters:

  • image (list[float]) – 2D array of projected Compton-y map around our galaxy of interest

  • center (list[float] or None) – The [x,y] pixel coordinates used as the center. Default is None, which useds the center of the image

Returns:

radial_prof-Radial profile of the azimuthally averaged signal

Return type:

list[float]

sz.make_radial_profiles(stamps, kernel, label, config, index_sample)¶

Convolves data and generates radial profiles for all galaxies in your sample

Parameters:

  • stamps (list[float]) – A nested array of the SZ data around galaxies. Output of cropping_sz function

  • kernel (float) – The standard deviation of the Gaussian kernel in units of pixels

  • label (str) – File name and path to save outputs

  • config (yaml) – input yaml files

Returns:

Saves an hdf5 file containing containing stacked radial profile and meta data about the sample

sz.topolar(img, order=1)¶

Transforms an image into polar coordinates

Parameters:

  • img (list[float]) – 2D data of the image wanting to transform

  • order (int) – The spline interpolation order, default 1

Returns:

Polar-Nested array of image by polar coordinates, (rads, angs)- Values of the radii and angles corresponding to the data in polar

Return type:

list[float]

sz.make_moment_profiles(stamps, kernel, label, config, index_sample)¶

Generates radial profiles of transformed maps for m=0, 1, and 2

Parameters:

  • stamps (list[float]) – A nested array of the SZ data around galaxies. Output of cropping_sz function

  • kernel (float) – The standard deviation of the Gaussian kernel in units of pixels

  • label (str) – File name and path to save outputs

  • config (yaml) – input yaml files

Returns:

Saves an hdf5 file containing containing stacked ratios of m1/m0 and m2/m0 and meta data about the sample (if not already included from radial analysis)

sz.thermal_energy(stamps, kernel, label, aperture, comov, z, theta, gal_sample_indices, config)¶

Calculates the thermal energy in a given aperature from maps of SZ-y data

Parameters:

  • stamps (list[float]) – A nested array of the SZ data around galaxies.

  • kernel (float) – The standard deviation of the Gaussian kernel in units of pixels

  • label (str) – File name and path to save outputs

  • aperture (float) – pixel size of the radius of the aperture within which you want to caculate thermal energy

  • comov (float) – comoving distance in Gpc

  • z (float) – Redshift of snapshot

  • theta (float) – pixel size in arcseconds

  • gal_sample_indices (list[float]) – indices of the galaxies in the catalog used to creat the stamps

  • config (yaml) – input yaml files

Returns:

Saves an hdf5 file containing thermal energy as a function of stellar and halo mass and meta data about the sample (if not already included from radial analysis)

sz.make_thermal_energy_plot(data, thresholds_stellar, thresholds_halo)¶

Generates data and error on thermal energy values for stacked galaxies

Parameters:

  • data (list[float]) – Nested list containing stellar masses of galaxies, halo masses of galaxies, and thermal energy around each galaxy

  • thresholds_stellar (list[float]) – Bin limits for stacking by stellar mass

  • thresholds_halo (list[float]) – Bin limits for stacking by halo mass

Returns:

y-Thermal energy of stacked galaxies in each stellar mass bin, err-Error on thermal energy of stacked galaxies in each stellar mass bin, y2$

Return type:

list[float]

sz.gen_random_indices(index_set, gen_size)¶

Generates a list of indicies by random sampling with replacement

Parameters:

  • index_set (list[float]) – List of values to sample from

  • gen_size (int) – Length of final resampled list you want

Returns:

a list of length gen_size randomly chosen from index_set

sz.single_catalog_bootstrap(data, boot_size, loop_size)¶

Calculates the means of a list of catalogs, useful when determining things like correlation matrices

Parameters:

  • data (list[float]) – List of pandas tables

  • boot_size (int) – Sample size

  • loop_size – How many times to do the bootstrapping

RAFIKI-CGM

Mock data of the hot CGM in cosmological simulations
